zotero.abstractNote: Gibbs sampling is a powerful tool for inference in probabilistic models, but it can struggle with high-dimensional, highly correlated distributions. We introduce Diffusive Gibbs Sampling (DGS), a framework that bridges Gibbs sampling and diffusion-based generative models. DGS augments the target distribution with a sequence of increasingly noisy versions of the variables, and performs Gibbs updates in this augmented space. This allows the sampler to traverse the state space more efficiently by leveraging the diffusive dynamics to escape local modes. We show that DGS can be interpreted as a form of Langevin dynamics that is compatible with Gibbs sampling, and we provide theoretical guarantees for its convergence. We demonstrate the effectiveness of DGS on a range of challenging inference tasks, including high-dimensional Bayesian logistic regression and deep latent variable models, where it outperforms standard Gibbs sampling and other MCMC baselines.
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Proceedings of the 41st International Conference on Machine Learning
